title: Heeria dispar
intro: Heeria dispar, also known as the Cape mahogany, is a plant species native to the Cape region of South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Heeria dispar include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Heeria dispar include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Heeria dispar is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It is a member of the genus Heeria, which consists of a small group of plants native to Africa. The plant is often grouped under the order Sapindales, a large order of flowering plants that includes many other families. Heeria dispar is further classified as a species within the Anacardiaceae family due to its unique characteristics and shared features with other members of the family. The classification of Heeria dispar reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other flowering plants.
